lesson.menu_desc: Used to / would (past habits) + healthy-lifestyle vocabulary -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 12 -- Habits Over Time -- Used To / Would & Past Habits
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Sandra Hayasaki | Aula 12 -- Habits Over Time

listenings.text: Let me tell you how my lifestyle changed over the last few years. I used to be a very different person. I used to drink three coffees a day, and I would stay up late every night watching television. I never used to work out. I had almost no energy in the mornings, and I felt tired all the time. Then, little by little, I decided to change one bad habit at a time. First, I cut down on coffee, from three cups to just one. Then I started to work out twice a week. I did not give up everything at once, because that never works for me. I changed gradually, step by step. Today I have so much more energy, and I feel younger than I did five years ago. The secret was simple: small changes, repeated every day, slowly became a whole new lifestyle.

I want to tell you about the habits I had when I was younger. When I was twenty, I used to live in a small apartment near the city center. Every weekend, I would walk to the market, buy fresh fruit, and cook for my friends. I used to have so much energy back then. I never used to worry about my health, because I was always moving. Over the years, my lifestyle changed. I sat more, I walked less, and slowly I lost some of that energy. Last year I decided to bring back one old habit. I gave up the elevator and started taking the stairs. I cut down on sugar, just a little at a time. I did not change everything overnight; I changed gradually. And do you know what? Some of that old energy came back. The girl who used to walk to the market is still inside me.

extra_audio.text: First, look honestly at one habit you want to change. Then choose to cut down slowly instead of giving up everything at once. Next, add one small healthy habit, like a short walk or a glass of water. After that, repeat it every day until it feels normal. Finally, notice your new energy and enjoy your healthier lifestyle.
